name: public_health_emergency_exemption_proposed_kestrel_st_lt
premise: A Public Health response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving public health emergency exemption proposed kestrel st lt. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is in the next hour. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as short term vs long term under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions. Ensure a usable human override/appeals path.

agents.name: Luc Mensah
agents.identity: I am Luc Mensah, accountable for a defensible call in Public Health when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays long term for short term.

agents.name: Mei Kim
agents.identity: I am Mei Kim, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Public Health.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so long term isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of short term.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect long term.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to long term to secure short term under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is in the next hour. Evidence is partial. Luc Mensah must decide under short term vs long term. What does Luc Mensah do?
